title: Tamil Thakkali Rasam

subs.name: 200 ml canned crushed tomatoes — add directly without extra water

ingredients.name: tamarind
visual_checks: just a small piece to add depth; the tomatoes provide most of the sourness
ingredients.notes: just a small piece to add depth; the tomatoes provide most of the sourness

subs.name: extra half tomato and a squeeze of lemon added off the heat
½ tsp black pepper powder + ½ tsp coriander powder + ¼ tsp cumin

steps.text: Soak the tamarind in 50 ml warm water, extract the pulp, and set aside. In a saucepan, combine the chopped tomatoes, tamarind extract, turmeric, and 600 ml water. Bring to a boil over medium-high flame.

Simmer on medium for 10 minutes until the tomatoes completely break down. Use the back of a ladle to press them against the pot and release all their juice into the broth. The liquid should turn deep orange-red.
Add the rasam powder, cracked black pepper, and salt. Stir and simmer for another 5 minutes. The rasam should be very thin and slightly foamy on top — that froth is a sign of a well-made rasam.
Heat oil in a small tempering pan. Splutter mustard seeds, then add garlic cloves and fry until golden. Add dried red chillies, curry leaves, and asafoetida and fry for 20 seconds.

Pour the tempering into the rasam and stir in coriander leaves. Taste for salt and tartness — a good thakkali rasam should be both sour and savoury. Serve hot over rice or in cups as a digestive soup.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
